---
title: "Organize Data Notebook"
---
#| label: load-data
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(modelsummary)
library(gt)
library(here)
library(fixest)

```{r}
#label: timestamp-start

timestamp()
```

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(here)
source(here("utils","check_packages.R"))
source(here("utils","functions.R"))
```

This quarto doc is used to organize the data.

# Load the Data

```{r}
# Load the data
RPS <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "RPS.xlsx"))
Renewable_Generation1 <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "Total Renewable energy production.xlsx"))
Renewable_Generation2 <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "Total Renewable energy production.xlsx"), sheet = 2)

```


```{r}
# checking basic structure
glimpse(RPS)
glimpse(Renewable_Generation1)
glimpse(Renewable_Generation2)
```

```{r}
# checking column names
names(RPS)
names(Renewable_Generation1)
names(Renewable_Generation2)
names(energy_data)
```

```{r}
# checking first few rows
head(RPS)
head(Renewable_Generation1)
head(Renewable_Generation2)
```

```{r}
# clean RPS
RPS <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "RPS.xlsx"), skip = 24)

RPS <- RPS |> 
  rename(state = State, rps_tier = `RPS Tier or Carve Out`) |>
  #  Total RPS
  filter(str_detect(rps_tier, "^Total RPS")) |>
  select(state, starts_with("20")) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("20"), names_to = "year", 
               values_to = "rps_total") |>
  mutate(year = as.numeric(year))

# clean 2: reverting states names from abbreviation to full name 
RPS <- RPS |>
  mutate(
    state = recode(state,
      !!!setNames(state.name, state.abb),
      "DC" = "District of Columbia"
    )
  )

```


```{r}
# cleaning renewable energy generation  
# fixing column names

# Extracting the second row as column names
names(Renewable_Generation1) <- as.character(
  Renewable_Generation1[2, ]
)

# Removing the first two rows
Renewable_Generation1 <- Renewable_Generation1[-c(1,2), ]

# for sheet 2
names(Renewable_Generation2) <- as.character(
  Renewable_Generation2[2, ]
)

# Remove the first two rows for sheet 2
Renewable_Generation2 <- Renewable_Generation2[-c(1,2), ]


# keep ultimate years from 2000 to 2023
Renewable_Generation1 <- Renewable_Generation1 |>
  select(State, any_of(as.character(2000:2023)))

Renewable_Generation2 <- Renewable_Generation2 |>
  select(State, any_of(as.character(2000:2023)))

```

```{r}
# merging RPS and renewable generation dataset
energy_data <- Renewable_Generation1 |>
  left_join(
    Renewable_Generation2,
    by = c("State", "year")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    percent_renewable = 100 * renewable_generation / total_generation,
    state = case_when(
      State == "DC" ~ "District of Columbia",
      TRUE ~ state.name[match(State, state.abb)]
    )
  ) |>
  select(
    state,
    year,
    renewable_generation,
    total_generation,
    percent_renewable
  )

# check
RPS_states <- unique(RPS$state)

energy_data <- energy_data |>
  filter(state %in% RPS_states)


# check
length(unique(energy_data$state))

setdiff(unique(RPS$state), unique(energy_data$state))

# final dataset 
final_data <- energy_data |>
  left_join(RPS, by = c("state", "year"))

# check final dataset
glimpse(final_data)

sum(is.na(final_data$rps_total))

setdiff(unique(RPS$state), unique(final_data$state))

# calculate RPS as percentage 
final_data <- final_data |>
  mutate(
    rps_percent = rps_total * 100
  )
```



```{r}
# save data
save(
  final_data,
  file = here(
    "data",
    "data_constructed",
    "analytical_data.RData"
  )
)
```


```{r}
# Descriptive Statistics
library(dplyr)

final_data |>
  summarise(
    mean_renewable = mean(percent_renewable, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_renewable = sd(percent_renewable, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rps = mean(rps_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rps = sd(rps_percent, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```


```{r}
# Descriptive statistics 2
datasummary_skim(
  final_data
)

# 
library(modelsummary)

datasummary_skim(
  final_data |> select(percent_renewable, rps_percent)
)
```

```{r}
# Descriptive Statistics 
final_data |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_renewable = mean(percent_renewable, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_renewable = sd(percent_renewable, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_renewable = min(percent_renewable, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_renewable = max(percent_renewable, na.rm = TRUE),

    mean_rps = mean(rps_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rps = sd(rps_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_rps = min(rps_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_rps = max(rps_percent, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```


```{r}
# Figure - PRS and renewable generation 
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  final_data,
  aes(
    x = rps_percent,
    y = percent_renewable
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Renewable Portfolio Standard (%)",
    y = "Renewable Generation Share (%)",
    title = "Relationship between RPS and Renewable Generation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```




```{r}
# Figure - boxplot 
ggplot(
  final_data,
  aes(
    x = factor(year),
    y = percent_renewable
  )
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Renewable Generation Share (%)",
    title = "Distribution of Renewable Generation Share by Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```

```{r}
# Figure 3
ggplot(
  final_data,
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = percent_renewable,
    group = state
  )
) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Renewable Generation Share (%)",
    title = "State-Level Renewable Generation Trends"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Basic OLS model; Bivariate Relationship
model1 <- lm(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent,
  data = final_data
)

summary(model1)
```


```{r}
# State Fixed Effect Model 
model2 <- lm(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent + factor(state),
  data = final_data
)

summary(model2)

#
library(fixest)

model2 <- feols(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent | state,
  data = final_data
)
```



```{r}
# model 3: state and year fixed effect
library(fixest)

model3 <- feols(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent |
    state + year,
  data = final_data
)

summary(model3)
```




```{r}
library(modelsummary)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "OLS" = model1,
    "State FE" = model2,
    "State + Year FE" = model4
  ),
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  stars = c("*" = .05, "**" = .01, "***" = .001),
  gof_omit = "AIC|BIC|Log.Lik"
)
```



```{r}
save(
  final_data,
  model1,
  model2,
  model3,
  file = here(
    "data",
    "data_constructed",
    "analytical_data.RData"
  )
)
```





# THIS SECTION BELOW IS FOR THE SHORTH TERM ANALYSIS i.e. 2008-2009
```{r}
# Loading data
rps <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "RPS.xlsx"))
generation <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "generation.xls"))
```


```{r}
# clean rps
rps <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "RPS.xlsx"), skip = 24)
rps <- rps |> 
  rename(state = State, rps_tier = `RPS Tier or Carve Out`) |>
  #  Total RPS
  filter(str_detect(rps_tier, "^Total RPS")) |>
  select(state, starts_with("20")) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("20"), names_to = "year", 
               values_to = "rps_total") |>
  mutate(year = as.numeric(year))

# clean 2: reverting states names from abbreviation to full name 
rps <- rps |>
  mutate(
    state = recode(state,
      !!!setNames(state.name, state.abb),
      "DC" = "District of Columbia"
    )
  )

# clean rps 3: Keep states that have rps data
states_keep <- c(
  "Arizona", "California", "Colorado", "Connecticut",
  "Delaware", "Hawaii", "Iowa", "Illinois", "Kansas",
  "Massachusetts", "Maryland", "Maine", "Michigan",
  "Minnesota", "Missouri", "Montana", "North Carolina",
  "Nebraska", "New Hampshire", "New Jersey", "New Mexico",
  "Nevada", "New York", "Ohio", "Oregon", "Pennsylvania",
  "Rhode Island", "Texas", "Virginia", "Washington",
  "Wisconsin", "District of Columbia"
)

rps <- rps |>
  filter(
    state %in% states_keep,
    year %in% c(2008, 2009)
  )
```


```{r}
# clean 1 renewable generation  
generation <- read_excel(here("data", "data_raw", "generation.xls"), skip = 6)
 

# clean 2
generation <- generation |>
  select(State, `2008`, `...3`, `2009`, `...8`) |>
  rename(
    state = State,
    total_generation_2008 = `2008`,
    percent_renewable_2008 = `...3`,
    total_generation_2009 = `2009`,
    percent_renewable_2009 = `...8`
  )


# clean 3: keep states that have RPS data avaialble
states_keep <- c(
  "Arizona",
  "California",
  "Colorado",
  "Connecticut",
  "DC",
  "Delaware",
  "Hawaii",
  "Iowa",
  "Illinois",
  "Kansas",
  "Massachusetts",
  "Maryland",
  "Maine",
  "Michigan",
  "Minnesota",
  "Missouri",
  "Montana",
  "North Carolina",
  "Nebraska",
  "New Hampshire",
  "New Jersey",
  "New Mexico",
  "Nevada",
  "New York",
  "Ohio",
  "Oregon",
  "Pennsylvania",
  "Rhode Island",
  "Texas",
  "Virginia",
  "Washington",
  "Wisconsin"
)

generation <- generation |>
  filter(state %in% states_keep)

sort(unique(generation$state))
```

```{r}
# merge rps and generation and craete final dateset 
energy_data <- rps |>
  left_join(
    generation_long,
    by = c("state", "year")
  )
```

```{r}
# handling missing values 
energy_data |>
  summarise(
    across(
      everything(),
      ~ sum(is.na(.))
    )
  )

# missing values 
energy_data |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) |>
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "n_missing"
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(n_missing))

#
energy_data |>
  filter(
    is.na(total_generation) |
    is.na(percent_renewable)
  )

# missing values 
energy_data <- energy_data |>
  drop_na(total_generation, percent_renewable)
```


```{r}
# chnage to percentage 
energy_data <- energy_data |>
  mutate(
    rps_percent = rps_total * 100
  )

```

```{r}
# save data
save(
  energy_data,
  file = here(
    "data",
    "data_constructed",
    "analytical_data.RData"
  )
)
```


```{r}
# Descriptive Statistics
library(dplyr)

energy_data |>
  summarise(
    mean_renewable = mean(percent_renewable),
    sd_renewable = sd(percent_renewable),
    mean_rps = mean(rps_percent),
    sd_rps = sd(rps_percent)
  )
```

```{r}
# Descriptive statistics 
datasummary_skim(
  energy_data
)
```



```{r}
ggplot(
  energy_data,
  aes(
    x = rps_total,
    y = percent_renewable
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Renewable Portfolio Standard",
    y = "Renewable Generation Share",
    title = "Relationship between RPS and Renewable Generation"
  )
```



```{r}
# Figure 2
ggplot(
  energy_data,
  aes(
    factor(year),
    percent_renewable
  )
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Renewable Generation Share"
  )
```

```{r}
# Basic OLS model; Bivariate Relationship
model1 <- lm(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent,
  data = energy_data
)
summary(model1)
```

```{r}
# State Fixed Effect Model 
model2 <- feols(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent | state,
  data = energy_data
)
summary(model2)
```



```{r}
# State + Year fixed effect 
model3 <- feols(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent | state + year,
  data = energy_data
)

summary(model3)
```



```{r}
# controling for total electricity generation (year + state + control)
model4 <- feols(
  percent_renewable ~ rps_percent + total_generation |
  state + year,
  data = energy_data
)

summary(model4)
```


```{r}
library(modelsummary)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "OLS" = model1,
    "State FE" = model2,
    "State + Year FE" = model3,
    "FE + Control" = model4
  )
)
```

```{r}
save(
  energy_data,
  model1,
  model2,
  model3,
  model4,
  file = here(
    "data",
    "data_constructed",
    "analytical_data.RData"
  )
)
```

```{r}
#label: timestamp-end

timestamp()
```
